name: nodejs-best-practices
description: Node.js development principles and decision-making. Framework selection, async patterns, security, and architecture. Teaches thinking, not copying.
capabilities: Guide framework selection based on project requirements (Edge, API, Enterprise, Full-stack)
Explain layered architecture principles (Controller, Service, Repository)
Provide async pattern guidance (async/await, Promise.all, Promise.allSettled)
Offer security checklist and validation principles
Help avoid common anti-patterns in Node.js development
author: sickn33
securitySummary: All 34 static findings are FALSE POSITIVES. This is pure documentation content (SKILL.md) containing educational material about Node.js best practices. The scanner misidentified ASCII tree diagrams (using backticks) as shell commands, security advice as vulnerabilities, and HTTP status code references as system reconnaissance. No executable code, network operations, or file operations are present.
valueStatement: Get guidance on Node.js architecture decisions, framework selection, async patterns, and security principles. This skill teaches decision-making frameworks rather than copy-paste code.
limitations: Does not write executable code - provides decision frameworks only
Does not have access to run Node.js or check runtime versions
Does not audit existing codebases for compliance
Does not provide live debugging or runtime analysis

useCases.title: New Project Architecture
useCases.description: Getting framework and architecture recommendations when starting a new Node.js project based on deployment target and requirements.

useCases.targetUser: Senior developers and tech leads conducting code reviews
useCases.title: Code Review Guidance
useCases.description: Using the decision checklist and anti-patterns section to review existing Node.js code for common issues.

useCases.title: Security Implementation
useCases.description: Applying the security checklist and validation principles when building secure Node.js applications.

promptTemplates.title: Framework Selection Help
promptTemplates.prompt: I am building a [type of application] with these requirements: [list requirements]. What framework should I use and why?
promptTemplates.scenario: Choosing between Express, Fastify, Hono, or NestJS for a new project

promptTemplates.title: Architecture Review
promptTemplates.prompt: Help me structure my Node.js application. It has [describe features]. Should I use a layered architecture?
promptTemplates.scenario: Designing application architecture for a growing project

promptTemplates.title: Async Pattern Selection
promptTemplates.prompt: I need to fetch data from [number] external APIs and then process the results. Which async pattern should I use?
promptTemplates.scenario: Choosing between async/await, Promise.all, or Promise.allSettled

promptTemplates.title: Security Checklist
promptTemplates.prompt: What security measures should I implement for a Node.js API that handles user authentication and payments?
promptTemplates.scenario: Applying security best practices to a production API

bestPractices: Ask users about their specific context before recommending solutions - do not assume one framework fits all needs
Consider the deployment target early in the decision process (Edge, Serverless, Container, VM)
Validate all inputs at API boundaries and treat all external data as untrusted
Use layered architecture for projects expected to grow beyond simple scripts
antiPatterns: Defaulting to Express for every project without considering alternatives
Using synchronous file operations (fs.readFileSync) in production code
Skipping input validation and trusting client data
Putting business logic directly in route handlers instead of service layer

faq.question: Which Node.js framework should I use in 2025?
faq.answer: It depends on your context. Use Hono for edge/serverless (fastest cold starts), Fastify for high-performance APIs (2-3x faster than Express), NestJS for enterprise teams needing structure, and Express for legacy projects or learning.

faq.question: Should I use TypeScript with Node.js?
faq.answer: Yes, TypeScript is recommended for Node.js projects. Node.js 22+ supports running .ts files directly with --experimental-strip-types. TypeScript provides better IDE support, catches errors at compile time, and improves maintainability.

faq.question: How do I handle errors in Node.js?
faq.answer: Use centralized error handling: create custom error classes, throw from any layer, catch at the top level (middleware), and format consistent responses. Clients get user-friendly messages with appropriate HTTP status codes while logs get full details.

faq.question: When should I use Promise.all vs Promise.allSettled?
faq.answer: Use Promise.all when all promises MUST succeed (fail fast). Use Promise.allSettled when you want all results regardless of individual failures. Use Promise.race for timeouts or first-response-wins scenarios.

faq.question: How do I secure my Node.js application?
faq.answer: Follow the security checklist: validate all inputs, use parameterized queries, hash passwords with bcrypt/argon2, verify JWTs, implement rate limiting, use security headers (Helmet), enforce HTTPS, and audit dependencies regularly.

faq.question: Can I use sync file operations in Node.js?
faq.answer: Never use synchronous file operations (fs.readFileSync, etc.) in production code. They block the event loop and degrade performance. Use async alternatives or offload to worker threads for CPU-intensive file processing.
